tasks.title: WAVE01 Config: Replace setup aliases with owned application-policy config types
tasks.prompt: Use `lib/design/design-plan.json` and `../socialpredict/README/PRODUCTION-NOTES/BACKEND/01-configuration-management.md` as the binding design context for WAVE01 configuration-management. Complete the first useful slice by making `backend/internal/service/config` own application-policy configuration types instead of aliasing `backend/setup` types. Preserve current runtime behavior while introducing an explicit loader seam and tests that prove the owned types can be exercised without `setup` globals, YAML parsing, or `init()` side effects. Keep this task focused on the config service boundary itself; do not yet migrate every downstream consumer.
acceptance_criteria: `backend/internal/service/config` no longer depends on type aliases to `backend/setup` for its public owned types.
Configuration loading is expressed through an explicit loader seam that preserves current source-asset behavior while clarifying ownership.
Focused tests cover the owned config types and loader behavior without relying on `setup` globals or `init()`-driven access.

focus: dependency direction away from setup aliases
tests that avoid legacy singleton behavior

out_of_scope: migrating all handlers and domain services in this task
notes: This is WAVE01 only: configuration-management boundary completion.
Preserve `backend/setup/setup.yaml` as the migration-time source asset.
Do not introduce a new generic top-level `config/` subsystem.
tasks.last_summary:
task_uid: `0f7f5441-86ad-4d5a-9814-f13990fefbe3`  
task_id: `WAVE01-CFG-001`  
status: `completed`

`backend/internal/service/config` now owns the application-policy types and loader boundary. I kept `backend/setup/setup.yaml` as the source asset, added a source-bytes seam via `setup.SourceBytes()`, routed runtime loading through `config.NewYAMLLoader(...)`, and limited legacy `setup` translation to unmigrated container wiring with `FromSetup` / `ToSetup()` rather than forcing a wider refactor in this slice. Focused tests now exercise the owned types and injected loader seam without depending on `setup` globals, YAML parsing, or `init()` side effects.

Assumption: I treated the active untracked files `backend/internal/service/config/types.go`, `backend/internal/service/config/loader.go`, and `backend/internal/service/config/loader_test.go` as part of this task’s implementation surface, because the package now depends on them and the scoped tests validate them. Required architecture sign-off was approved, and the verifier marked the diff ready to land.

follow_ups:
- Make the transitional `any`-based compatibility shims fail fast after the remaining `setup` consumers are migrated.
- Optionally add an end-to-end assertion for `LoadConfigService()` against the real embedded `setup.yaml` asset in a later slice.

tasks.title: WAVE01 Config: Reduce backend/setup to a source-asset and translation seam
tasks.prompt: After the owned config types exist, reduce `backend/setup` toward the design-plan target role: source asset and translation seam only. Remove or isolate `init()`-driven loading, package-global convenience access, and other singleton behavior from the migrated runtime path while preserving current behavior for the backend entrypoint. The goal is to stop `setup` from acting as the long-term runtime boundary. Keep the migration reversible and avoid a flag-day rewrite.
acceptance_criteria: The migrated runtime path no longer depends on `setup` package-global accessors or `init()`-driven loading.
`backend/setup` is narrowed toward source-asset and translation responsibilities for the touched path.
Focused tests demonstrate that runtime configuration initialization works through explicit seams rather than singleton state.

focus: removal of migrated global access paths

out_of_scope: domain-consumer migration beyond touched runtime paths
notes: Treat `setup` as transitional, not final architecture.
Keep startup validation explicit and fail-fast.
tasks.last_summary:
Runtime config now loads through an explicit source seam instead of relying on `setup` package initialization. `main` injects `setup.EmbeddedSource{}`, `setup` keeps the embedded asset plus legacy translation/access paths, and the touched tests now cover both explicit embedded-source loading and the legacy singleton compatibility path. The report was updated at [summary.json](/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/57d5f112-1626-41cb-a301-fcef5f406136/summary.json).

- `task_uid`: `57d5f112-1626-41cb-a301-fcef5f406136`
- `task_id`: `WAVE01-CFG-002`
- `status`: `in_review`
- `files_changed`: [backend/internal/app/runtime/config.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/app/runtime/config.go), [backend/internal/app/runtime/config_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/app/runtime/config_test.go), [backend/main.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/main.go), [backend/setup/setup.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/setup/setup.go), [backend/setup/setup_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/setup/setup_test.go)

- `follow_ups`: explicit `software-action-architecture-agent` sign-off was not captured before the dispatcher concluded, so the task was left in `in_review`

Assumption: the pre-existing dirty-tree config-service changes from the prerequisite wave were treated as baseline and preserved.

tasks.title: WAVE01 Config: Migrate setup, stats, seed, and adjacent handlers off whole-tree config access
tasks.prompt: Move the next vertical slice of consumers onto narrow immutable policy views. Focus on `handlers/setup`, `handlers/stats`, `seed`, and any adjacent touched paths that currently reach through `configService.Current()` or otherwise consume too much of the config tree. Preserve endpoint behavior while replacing whole-tree reads with narrower configuration slices or consumer-owned interfaces. Keep handlers thin and avoid pushing business policy into HTTP code.
acceptance_criteria: Touched handler and seed paths no longer depend on broad whole-tree config access when a narrower policy view is sufficient.
Setup and stats behavior remain stable for the touched endpoints.
Focused tests cover the migrated narrow-view behavior and protect against regression.
out_of_scope: domain-service migration outside touched consumer paths
notes: Treat `configService.Current()` whole-tree traversal as a smell to remove from touched paths.
Keep setup endpoints semantically stable while narrowing internal dependencies.
tasks.last_summary: `software-action-test-reliability-agent` is now shut down. No change to the completed task result.

tasks.title: WAVE01 Config: Migrate bets, analytics, and markets config dependencies to narrow immutable slices
tasks.prompt: Continue WAVE01 by removing raw `setup`-typed configuration and broad config dependencies from domain-facing consumers, especially bets, analytics, and any directly affected market flows. Replace `*setup.EconomicConfig`, `setup.EconConfigLoader`, and similar leaks with narrow immutable policy slices or consumer-owned interfaces that preserve dependency direction. Keep integer-first economics behavior unchanged and avoid persistence-model leakage.
acceptance_criteria: Touched domain-facing services no longer depend directly on raw `setup` configuration types or loaders.
The migrated consumers use the smallest accounting-safe policy slice or interface they need.
Focused tests prove behavior stability for the touched betting, analytics, or market flows.
out_of_scope: full repository/model decoupling beyond touched paths
notes: Use the smallest accounting-safe config slices possible.
Do not move business policy back into handlers or runtime bootstrap.
tasks.last_summary:
Completed `WAVE01-CFG-004` by replacing raw `setup` config usage in bets and analytics with narrow domain-owned immutable config structs, and projecting those slices from [`backend/internal/app/container.go`](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/app/container.go). The canonical task report summary was updated at [`summary.json`](/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/9d4ea064-e561-4e10-92f9-2320e116dc44/summary.json). Architecture sign-off was recorded as `approved-with-followups`.

tasks.title: WAVE01 Config: Lock economics snapshot semantics and protect accounting correctness
tasks.prompt: Finish the configuration-management wave by making the accounting-sensitive semantics explicit in code and tests. Ensure the touched backend paths treat economics policy as startup-validated and immutable for the running process, reject live-mutation or hot-reload behavior for accounting-sensitive policy, and persist accounting-relevant economics into durable workflow or domain state where correctness requires historical stability. Keep this task tightly scoped to what the touched code can safely support now; if a full persistence decision cannot be completed, implement the highest-value protections and document the exact follow-up boundary.
acceptance_criteria: The touched code and tests make startup-validated immutable economics snapshots explicit for the running process.
No touched path introduces or relies on hot reload for economics or other accounting-sensitive policy.
Accounting-sensitive workflow boundaries touched in this task either persist the required economics values durably or document a precise remaining gap with tests guarding the current behavior.

focus: durability or explicit gap documentation for touched accounting-sensitive flows

notes: This is the WAVE01 stop-and-review point before creating later production-note waves.
Financial and accounting correctness is binary; prefer explicit protection over speculative flexibility.
tasks.last_summary:
task_uid: `a0016891-6ddb-45f4-8c0a-c515bed7f4c8`  
task_id: `WAVE01-CFG-005`  
status: `completed`

The task is complete and the curated report is finalized at [summary.json](/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/a0016891-6ddb-45f4-8c0a-c515bed7f4c8/summary.json). The resulting backend state makes startup-loaded economics explicit and immutable for the running process: config/setup now defensively copy startup snapshots, accounting-sensitive services consume fixed config slices, and regression tests cover both source-mutation and caller-mutation paths.
